onboarding.shortcutHint: Tip: press ? for shortcuts, ⌘K for the command palette

overview.title: Welcome back
overview.tagline: Full botnet visibility · live data from hub
overview.noActivityDesc: Operator actions (user/ban changes, config edits), bot connect/disconnect, and owner chat appear here. Channel IRC events don't surface here — check the channel console for those.

channels.title: Channels
channels.name: Name
channels.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete channel {name}?
channels.deleteConfirmBody: Delete channel {name}? Bots will leave immediately.
channels.bulkDeleteConfirmBody: Delete {n} selected channels? Bots will leave each of them immediately.
channels.opMjoinDesc: Make all bots join this channel (.mjoin)
channels.opMpartDesc: Make all bots part this channel (.mpart)
channels.opMcycleDesc: Make all bots leave and rejoin (.mcycle)
channels.confirmMcycle: All bots will leave and rejoin {chan}. Users on the channel will see them reconnect.
channels.confirmMpart: All bots will leave {chan}. The channel will lose bot management until you rejoin them.
channels.opPerBotDesc: Run join / part / cycle on a single specific bot
channels.perBotDesc: Pick a bot to run a single-bot action — useful when you want to add or remove just one bot from this channel without affecting the others.
channels.opRjoinDesc: Make this bot join the channel (.rjoin)
channels.opRpartDesc: Make this bot leave the channel (.rpart)
channels.opRcycleDesc: Make this bot leave and rejoin (.rcycle)
channels.joinAllHint: Adds the channel to the hub userlist — every bot joins now and will rejoin after restarts.
channels.joinSelectedHint: Sends .rjoin to chosen bots. No userlist entry — bots will not rejoin after restart.
channels.noOnlineBots: No bots online — selective mode unavailable.
channels.joinDelayHintAll: 0 = all bots join simultaneously. Higher = staggered .mjoin every N seconds.
channels.joinDelayHintSelected: 0 = .rjoin fires for every bot immediately. Higher = each bot delayed by N seconds.

users.title: Users
users.name: Handle
users.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete user {name}?
users.deleteConfirmBody: Delete user {name}? This cannot be undone.
users.bulkDeleteConfirmBody: Delete {n} selected users? This cannot be undone.
users.addrHint: IP addresses / classes (e.g. 92.206.50.* for a dynamic IP class). Checked independently of hosts — add one here when IRC masks aren't enough, e.g. after a class change.
users.matchSearchHint: Runs .match — shows handles matching the mask, optionally filtered by flags and channel.
users.matchTimeout: No response — is the hub up?
users.renameDesc: Renaming a handle updates the hub userlist and replicates to all bots immediately. Any active session for this user will be dropped.
users.renameInvalid: Handle must be 1–16 alphanumeric characters with no spaces.
users.renameSameHandle: New handle is the same as old.
users.resetChanFlagsBody: Restores {handle}'s flags for {chan} from offence history (.rflags). Only works if the user has logged offences on that channel.

bots.title: Bots
bots.name: Name

type.leafDesc: Connects to the hub; does not accept links from other bots.
type.mainDesc: Central hub that owns the userlist and coordinates the botnet.

bots.linkPasswordHint: Used by the bot to authenticate when linking to this hub (.chpass).
bots.planPreview: Hub commands that will be sent
bots.errInvalidHandle: 1–9 chars, must start with a letter, no spaces.
bots.errInvalidIp: Enter a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address.
bots.invalidNick: Invalid IRC nick. Max 30 chars, first char must be a letter or one of []\`_^{|}, the rest may also include digits and hyphens.
bots.nickChangeLive: Nick changed live: {nick}. Config save runs automatically.
bots.nickChangeQueued: Bot offline — nick {nick} will apply on next IRC connection.
bots.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete bot {name}?
bots.deleteConfirmBody: Delete bot {name}? This cannot be undone.
bots.bulkDeleteConfirmBody: Delete {n} selected bots? This cannot be undone.
bots.configDesc: Runs .bc <handle> cfg on the bot. Changes apply immediately; Save writes to disk.
bots.configDescAuto: Changes are saved to disk automatically after 2s of inactivity. Multi-value entries (server, alt, listen…) support add/remove per row.
bots.noConfigDesc: The bot returned no cfg entries. Make sure you have +x flag globally.
bots.configFetchTimeout: No response from bot — is it online?
bots.settingsDesc: Runtime .set variables — flood thresholds, timeouts, network params. Changes persist automatically via userlist save.
bots.noSettingsDesc: The bot returned no set entries. Make sure you have +x flag globally.
bots.statusDesc: Live bot status — uptime, IRC connection, lag, channel list. Manual refresh.
bots.statusEmpty: Bot returned no status — is it online and do you have +x?
bots.statusFetchTimeout: No response — bot may have disconnected.
bots.modulesDesc: Loaded modules of this bot. Module management is done through config (cfg load <file>) and requires rehash or restart.
bots.modulesEmptyDesc: The module list is empty. Load a module via cfg +load name.so in the Configuration tab.
bots.modulesFetchTimeout: No response — is the bot online?
bots.rehashDesc: Reload config and modules without restart.
bots.rehashConfirmBody: The bot will reload its config file and modules. IRC connection stays, partyline session may be reset.
bots.restartDesc: Restart the bot — it shuts down and comes back automatically.
bots.restartConfirmBody: Bot will disconnect and reconnect within seconds. One-shot operation — bot returns with current config.
bots.dieDesc: Kill bot process (does NOT come back on its own).
bots.dieConfirmBody: Bot will be killed and will NOT return automatically. You must manually start it on the host. Destructive operation.
bots.lifecycleDesc: Operations on the live bot process. Require +x flag.
bots.upAllDesc: Try to connect all offline bots (.upbots).
bots.upAllConfirmBody: Hub will try to connect all offline bots. Online bots are untouched.
bots.downAllConfirmBody: All online bots will be cut off from the hub. DESTRUCTIVE — channels temporarily lose handling until bots return.

telegram.title: Telegram notifier
telegram.subtitle: Alert service status and deployment configuration
telegram.statusTitleRunning: Notifier is running and connected to the hub
telegram.statusTitleNotReady: Notifier is not ready to send alerts
telegram.statusDescRunning: The systemd service is active, authenticated to the hub WebAPI, and received the initial bot list.
telegram.statusDescActiveNoAuth: The process is active, but recent logs do not show auth_ok. Check the password, +PN flags, and HUB_SSL.
telegram.statusDescStopped: The service is stopped or not installed yet. Use the configuration below only when it needs to be deployed again.
telegram.messageSectionHint: The text is sent through the notifier configuration to the current alerts chat. The endpoint requires an active panel session.
telegram.messageAuthMissing: No active panel session token — log in again
telegram.messageNotifierOffline: Notifier must be running and connected to the hub
telegram.botSectionHint: Paste the token from @BotFather. It's validated live via the Telegram API — the "Validate" button calls /getMe and shows the bot name if the token is good. The token never leaves this page (no localStorage, no backend round-trip).
telegram.tokenShape: Format looks wrong — BotFather tokens are digits, a colon, and ~35 alphanumeric characters. E.g. 1234567890:ABCdef…
telegram.chatSectionHint: Paste the chat_id. Supergroups start with -100…; DMs are positive. The "Send test" button pushes a short message to that chat — if it arrives, the bot has permission to post.
telegram.chatShape: Chat ID is a number, optionally negative for groups
telegram.chatHint: Easiest method: in the group, forward any message to @getidsbot — it replies with the chat ID.
telegram.handleSectionHint: The notifier logs into the hub as its own read-only service user with +P partyline access and +N notify/monitoring access. Pick a handle + password and the wizard generates the partyline commands.
telegram.partylineHint: Open partyline on the hub and paste these 4 lines one by one:
telegram.deploySectionHint: Config is ready. Paste the generated .env.notifier and start the process. SSH is required here — token and password can't travel through a browser to our server (would be less secure than SSH).
telegram.envFileHint: Copy the whole block and save it as .env.notifier on the host running the notifier:
telegram.allReady: All set — running these commands will start the notifier
telegram.completeStepsDesc: Token must validate, test message must succeed, password must be filled

hubSettings.title: Hub settings
hubSettings.subtitle: Global partyline configuration (.set / .gset / .dset)
hubSettings.fetchTimeout: Hub didn't respond — check if you have +s
hubSettings.resetConfirmBody: Send .{name} with no value? The hub will revert this variable to its default (or clear it for string types).
hubSettings.readonlyNotice: Read-only — editing .set/.gset/.dset requires the +s global flag.

ircPresence.noOnlineBotDesc: To view IRC presence, at least one bot must be online — .bc <bot> cwho runs from its perspective.
ircPresence.permissionDenied: Bot denied — requires +n on channel or globally
ircPresence.fetchTimeout: Bot didn't respond — it may not be on the channel
ircPresence.emptyDesc: Bot {bot} sees an empty channel — it may not be synced here.

channelMember.noOnlineBot: No online bot — none can send MODE/KICK

confirm.title: Confirm deletion
confirm.deleteProtlist: Are you sure you want to remove entry {mask} from the protection list?
confirm.deleteHost: Are you sure you want to remove host {host}?
confirm.deleteAddr: Are you sure you want to remove address {addr}?

intro.title: Operations Hub
intro.tagline: IRC botnet control. Quiet, from a distance, in one place.

topology.title: Botnet topology
topology.noIssuesDesc: Every group has all bots connected.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.emptyDesc: Changes made on the botnet will appear here as they happen.
audit.noMatches: No events match the current filters
audit.clearConfirmDesc: This removes all captured events from this browser. Other owners' logs are unaffected.

sessionChanges.title: My session edits
sessionChanges.hint: Every value you changed since you opened this tab. Cleared on refresh — use the global audit log for permanent history.
sessionChanges.clearConfirmDesc: Removes the in-memory list of edits made from this tab. The actual changes on the botnet are not reverted.

objectHistory.title: Recent changes
objectHistory.emptyDesc: No audit events captured for this object yet. Changes made from any owner's panel or partyline will appear here.

boundary.title: Something broke in this view
boundary.hint: The rest of the panel is still working. Try again, or switch to another view.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Press ? to toggle this overlay · Esc to close

irc.title: IRCNet servers
irc.subtitle: Known IRCNet servers and our bot coverage
irc.noMatches: No servers match the current filter
irc.allCoveredDesc: Every server in the directory has at least one of your bots. Nothing to add here.
irc.noConnectedDesc: No bot from your botnet is online on any known IRCNet server right now.
irc.userDataUnavailable: Live user counts not available — configure your proxy to reach an IRCNet stats source
irc.cacheNote: External data cached {age}s ago · refreshes every 5 min
irc.sourceUnavailable: IRCNet server source (ircnet.info) temporarily unreachable — the list may be incomplete. Try refreshing shortly.

presence.title: Online admins
presence.bootConfirmBody: They will be immediately disconnected from the partyline. This does NOT remove them from the userlist — they can reconnect.

idiots.title: Idiots list
idiots.desc: Host patterns marked as habitual troublemakers. Used by channel protection to progressively remove flags (0-5 severity).
idiots.emptyDesc: No host has been added to the idiot list. Hosts are typically added here when they repeatedly disrupt channels.
idiots.noMatches: No entries match the current search
idiots.deniedDesc: Hub rejected .idiots — needs read access to the 'idiots' pseudo-handle. Usually requires +s/+x globally.
idiots.tmpCantRemove: Temporary entry — auto-expires, cannot be removed manually
idiots.addrsDesc: Idiots are also matched by IP. These entries are added automatically when a host hits the threshold.
idiots.truncatedHint: Hub responded but we never saw the hosts: header. The connection may have been cut mid-reply, or your hub build has a modified format. Try Refresh.

bans.title: Bans & protlists
bans.subtitle: Ban, stick, exempt, invite, reop — per channel and global.
bans.subtitleGlobal: Global entries (shit) — applied to every synced channel.
bans.pickChannelDesc: Select a channel above or "Global" to view ban lists.
bans.emptyListDesc: This channel has no entries for the selected type.
bans.addGlobalHint: Add an entry to the global list (applied to all synced channels)
bans.globalFetching: Scraping global entries from the bot (.bans *)…
bans.globalFetched: Global entries fetched {age}s ago · click refresh to update
bans.globalNotLoaded: Global entries not loaded yet — click refresh
bans.stickGlobalUnavailable: The bot doesn't expose "stick" globals separately — see the Bans tab (entries with [*] are stickies).

offences.title: Offences
offences.desc: Per-user offence history tracked by the bot (idiot-level progression). Use 'Clear all' to reset the counter.
offences.emptyDesc: This user has no recorded offences.
offences.confirmClear: Reset the offence history for {user}. This cannot be undone.

verify.title: Verify botnet consistency
verify.desc: Runs .verify to check userlist and protection-list consistency across all connected bots. Expect 10–60 s on a large botnet — each bot has to walk its full user/protlist and compare checksums. An empty result means everything is in sync.

userNotes.title: Notes
userNotes.desc: Free-form key/value notes for {user}. Useful for shared admin context — emails, contact info, history.
userNotes.emptyDesc: No metadata recorded for this user.
userNotes.emptyDescAdmin: No metadata recorded yet. Add notes to share context with other admins.

massKick.title: Mass kick on {chan}
massKick.btnDesc: Kick everyone matching a tier from this channel
massKick.warning: Destructive operation — kicks multiple users at once. Use during incidents only.

a.name: Everyone
a.desc: Kick all users from the channel

massKick.lockDesc: Activate op-lockdown so kicked users can't immediately rejoin

bottree.title: Botnet hierarchy
bottree.desc: Tree view derived from .bt — shows slave/leaf relations across the botnet.
bottree.emptyDesc: No bottree output captured. The bot may not have responded yet, or the format wasn't recognized.

health.title: Health
health.subtitle: One-look botnet status
health.heroDetailHealthy: No issues across hub, bots, IRC, lockdowns, or recent events.
health.heroDetailIssues: {n} item(s) need attention. Click below to investigate.

issues.wsDisconnectDetail: Panel cannot reach the hub. Check the proxy and reverse proxy.
issues.lockdownDetail: Channel is in protection mode — open it to review or disable.
issues.netsplitDetail: Bot's wasoptest cache will auto-restore ops on rejoin if `set wasoptest ON`.

massBan.title: Mass ban · paste list
massBan.warning: Batch operation — each line becomes a separate ban on the channel. Review the preview before executing.
massBan.kickNeedsChannel: Ban + kick requires a specific channel
massBan.targetsPlaceholder:
nick1
spammer!*@*
*!*@bad.proxy.com
# comments and blank lines are skipped
massBan.targetsHelp: Naked nicks become `nick!*@*`, naked hosts become `*!*@host`, full masks are used as-is. Duplicates are removed.

matrix.title: Bot × Channel matrix
matrix.descLive: Live presence from .list c — single broadcast covers every bot. Cells show ops / joined / syncing / configured-but-absent.
matrix.readonly: Matrix requires +n — .list c is gated on the hub side.
matrix.emptyDesc: Bring at least one bot online to see channel presence.
matrix.fetchFailed: Matrix fetch failed — check the connection and try again.
matrix.noChans: No bot is in any channel
matrix.noChansDesc: Check that mjoin/rjoin ran, or that the bots have channels in their config for auto-join.
matrix.cellAbsent: {bot} is configured for {chan} but NOT joined (rjoin gap)
matrix.colOpsBad: None of the present bots have ops on this channel
matrix.colCoverage: {joined} of {total} online bots present on this channel

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.desc: Desktop notifications shown when the panel tab isn't focused. Your browser must allow them and not be in do-not-disturb mode.
notifications.unsupported: Your browser doesn't support desktop notifications.
notifications.blocked: Notifications are blocked at the browser level. Unblock them in site settings and come back here.
notifications.masterDesc: Master switch. When off no notifications are dispatched.
notifications.kindBotOfflineDesc: Notify when a previously online bot disconnects.
notifications.kindBotOnlineDesc: Notify when an offline bot reconnects to the hub.
notifications.kindMentionDesc: Notify when someone types your handle or one of the keywords below.
notifications.keywordsDesc: Extra terms that trigger a mention notification. Your own handle is matched automatically.
notifications.testDesc: Fire a sample notification to verify OS-level settings.

quickban.title: Quick ban
quickban.blurb: A ban is automatically applied on the channel (sets +b, kicks matches). Stick prevents auto-removal.
